hypothesis: If a generative model undergoes combined transfer learning by pre-training on large chemical databases (ChEMBL and ZINC-15), fine-tuning on a curated Covalent Triazine Framework dataset, and reinforcement learning with expert-defined rewards for properties like flatness, symmetry, conjugation, steric hindrance, and pattern matching, then it will yield generated CTF structures with higher validity, uniqueness, internal diversity, novelty, and proportion passing expert filters (and lower Fréchet ChemNet Distance) than models that omit one or more of these components, because such transfer learning provides domain-relevant representations and reward-guided optimization tailored to CTF design.
